contest.title: Codeforces Round 1092 (Unrated, Div. 1, Based on THUPC 2026 Finals)

problem.title: EXPloration, EXPloitation, and Gain Some EXPerience!

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: two-player row jumping game, total n <= 1e5, weights up to 1e9.
Used to confirm the distance >= 6 blocking lemma and backward DP over leading position, gap, side-to-move, and mask.
Luogu article cross-checks the same bounded-gap and small-mask DP conclusion.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution index; the article listed in sources was the actually consulted Luogu source.

annotation.summary: Two players move pieces forward on a weighted line, blackening landed cells and gaining weight; compute Shiro's final score minus White's under optimal play.
annotation.constraints: Total n <= 1e5, weights up to 1e9; a linear number of constant-size game states is intended.
annotation.core_idea: If the leading piece is at least 6 positions ahead and it is the leader's turn, the leader can move by 1 repeatedly to create a four-cell barrier that the trailing piece cannot cross. This turns far-apart states into a closed-form suffix outcome. Otherwise the game state is local: leader position, gap <= 5, side to move, and an occupancy mask for cells between the pieces. A minimax DP over score difference evaluates all legal moves.

solution_variants.name: bounded-gap-minimax-dp
solution_variants.summary: Use the blocking lemma to cap relevant gaps at 5. Memoize transitions over leading/trailing positions, side to move, and a bitmask of blackened cells between them; evaluate moves as immediate gain minus opponent continuation.

tags.evidence: Every nonterminal state is represented by constant-size local information per position: gap <= 5, side-to-move, and bounded occupancy mask.
The game is zero-sum on score difference; each transition maximizes current gain minus the opponent continuation value.
The main insight collapses the unbounded visited-position game into equivalent bounded-frontier states plus a closed-form suffix.
The DP stores the blackened status of at most four relevant cells between the two pieces as a bitmask.
